title: Technical disparity brief (methods, findings, limitations; non-stigmatizing)
context: With the disparity analysis complete (disparities-analysis-disparity-010), this task produces the technical disparity brief for a researcher and health-equity advocate audience. The brief must state methods, findings, and limitations in non-stigmatizing, evidence-tiered framing anchored to the editorial policy (disparities-analysis-framing-001) and must pass citation-coverage and framing review. No ranking/scorecard framing. Depends on: disparities-analysis-disparity-010.
objective: Write the technical disparity brief for the exemplar cancer site targeting a researcher/health-equity advocate audience. The brief states findings with mandatory caveats (data vintage, suppression, confidence intervals, 'association is not causation'/ecological-fallacy, 'adjusted-for / not-adjusted-for') and uses evidence-tiered structural framing: distinguishing 'this analysis measures the gap' from 'the established literature attributes such gaps to structural factors X/Y (cite)'. Intersectional cuts suppression forced out are explicitly disclosed. No ranking/scorecard framing. Every quantitative claim is sourced. Passes framing review.
acceptanceCriteria: States methods clearly: data sources (with licenses), age-standardization method, CI method, joinpoint trend approach, disparity measures used (absolute + relative + index of disparity), suppression thresholds applied, and race/ethnicity misclassification caveats

States findings with mandatory caveats on every quantitative output: data vintage and release year, small-cell suppression, confidence intervals, ecological-fallacy/'association is not causation' caveat, and the one-line 'adjusted-for / not-adjusted-for' note

Uses evidence-tiered structural framing throughout: 'this analysis measures the gap' statements are distinguished from 'the established literature attributes such gaps to structural factors X/Y (cite)' statements — no over-claiming causation from purely ecological data
Explicitly discloses intersectional cuts that suppression forced out of the analysis rather than silently omitting them
No ranking/scorecard framing, 'worst places' language, or naming-and-shaming of any community, provider, or institution
Every quantitative assertion carries a source reference; citation-coverage CI check passes
Methods reviewer and framing reviewer sign-off recorded confirming statistical accuracy and non-stigmatizing framing
output: A reviewed technical disparity brief document (for researcher/advocate audience) with methods, findings, limitations, evidence-tiered structural framing, and mandatory caveats — methods-reviewed and framing-reviewed, with sign-offs recorded.
